caveats: Loot tables, contested-zone drops and shop stock change between patches and server wipes — treat specific drop locations, prices and rarities as 4.8-era and verify in-game.
Acquisition is community-sourced where marked COMMUNITY (player guides/reports); CONFIRMED means corroborated by the SC Wiki or official sources.
Player-marketplace prices (UEX/sc-market) are unofficial and highly volatile.
Unofficial — not affiliated with Cloud Imperium Games. Data: Star Citizen Wiki (CC BY-SA), UEX Corp, community guides.

entries.name: Aril
entries.notableFor: Greycat Industrial's debut armor and the very first industrial medium set in the 'verse — a working miner's rig rather than a combat suit. Its signature look is the fully enclosed helmet paired with a tall protective collar that braces the neck and back of the head, plus an integrated tool belt and clip-on backpack that keep a worker's hands free for the job. The base finish is bright blue-and-white with grey accents (the Hazard variant swaps in high-visibility safety yellow), chosen so crews stand out on a jobsite. Lore credits the design to Greycat engineer Ruben Pardue, drafted after the Uriel tunnel collapse to add new safety features and advanced alloy plating; it became Greycat's enduring bestseller. The styling deliberately echoes the Pyro RYT multi-tool.

acquisition.where: Providence Surplus, Providence Platform, Orison (Crusader, Stanton)
acquisition.location: Buyable in-game with aUEC; the Aril Core (torso) lists around 5,168 aUEC, with the matching helmet, backpack, arms and legs sold as separate pieces. Pressurized medium set, so it works for EVA/industrial use.

acquisition.where: In-game crafting (blueprint)
acquisition.location: The base Aril set and its pieces can also be crafted in Alpha 4.x rather than bought, per the wiki availability data.

acquisition.where: RSI Pledge Store (real-money cosmetic variants)
acquisition.location: Special colorways — Black Cherry, Harvester, Quicksilver and Red Alert — are sold as standalone pledge/store items and are not the standard in-game purchase.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Aril (armor set)
Star Citizen Wiki — Aril Core (torso, manufacturer/components)
Cornerstone Finder — Aril Core (shop: Providence Surplus, Orison; ~5,168 aUEC)
UEX — Aril Core item / pricing

entries.name: Artimex
entries.notableFor: A clean, modern medium combat suit that captures the signature Quirinus look: tightly detailed plating built for both form and function, a fully enclosed helmet with a glowing neon ocular accent, a cowl that fans out over the helmet, and a knee-length fitted cape. It is pressurized and unisex, and shows up in an unusually large spread of liveries — the grey-with-gold base, plus Canuto, Lodestone, Wildwood, Hurston Dynamics Executive Security, Chairman's Club, Red Alert and the bright-white Starchaser. Originally teased back in 2018 as Hurston Security armor before finally shipping in Alpha 3.15.1.

acquisition.where: In-game loot crates (the base grey/gold Artimex set and several liveries drop here)
acquisition.location: Found in loot crates across the persistent universe

acquisition.where: Craftable from a blueprint (base variant)
RSI Pledge Store — sold as standalone livery packs for Canuto, Lodestone, Wildwood, Red Alert and Starchaser variants (real money)
Red Alert livery introduced at CitizenCon 2952 in the 'Red Alert' Weapons & Armor Collection; Starchaser released for IAE 2954
Chairman's Club livery was a one-time reward to all backers who held Concierge status before Alpha 4.2.1

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Artimex (armor set)
Star Citizen Wiki — Artimex Core (loot crates, crafting)
RSI Pledge Store — Quirinus Tech Artimex 'Red Alert' Armor
RSI Pledge Store — Quirinus Tech Artimex 'Starchaser' Armor

entries.name: Aztalan
entries.notableFor: A small-production light armor set that pairs hard plating with reinforced polymer weaves to shield vital areas while keeping the wearer fast and mobile. Its claim to fame is in-fiction pop culture: in 2946 the set was worn by the lead character Déraciné in the hit Spectrum show "Shadow Siren," turning a niche maker into a trending fad and giving the armor its frontier-chic, screen-icon reputation. The look leans subtle and rugged rather than military, with a slim profile and storage for personal and mission gear.

acquisition.where: RSI Subscriber Store (Subscriber Flair). Released as June 2021 / June 2951 in-fiction subscriber flair; obtainable by active subscribers, plus account-bound resale on third-party gray markets.
acquisition.location: RSI website Subscriber Store / pledge account

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Aztalan
Star Citizen Wiki - Tehachapi (manufacturer)
RSI Comm-Link - June 2951 Subscriber Promotions

entries.name: Bastion
entries.notableFor: A sleek, low-profile light armor set built around a clean military-foundation aesthetic. The wiki frames it as Greycat's affordable industrial "safety harness" line — a reinforced composite harness and pads meant to keep workers protected in harsh environments — which gives it that utilitarian, no-frills look. What makes it notable for collectors is its lineage: the asset carries an Odin Munitions Corporation (OMC) tag in its in-game item ID, marking it as a Squadron 42 / Odin Munitions livery piece rather than a normal PU shop item. It pairs with the Foundation Undersuit and is fully pressurized with balanced all-around resistances (roughly even physical/energy/thermal/distortion/biochemical protection) plus strong radiation scrubbing — a competent jack-of-all-trades light kit. The set comprises the Bastion Helmet, Core, Arms and Legs over the Foundation Undersuit.

acquisition.where: Not obtainable in the live PU. The Bastion set was datamined (first surfaced around Alpha 3.12.0) and remains an unreleased asset tied to Squadron 42 / Odin Munitions, with no shop, loot site, contested-zone source, event reward, or crafting recipe associated with it. There is no confirmed in-game acquisition path as of Alpha 4.8.x.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Bastion (armor set)
Star Citizen Wiki - Bastion Core
Star Citizen Wiki - Foundation Undersuit

entries.name: CC's Conversions Azrael 'Sapphire' Helmet (Subscriber flair)
entries.notableFor: April 2026 subscriber flair - a flight helmet with a grim skull visible beneath the visor, bundled in the 'Contractor's Kit' with a PSX Pistol Case. Subscriber-exclusive cosmetic, not normally purchasable.

acquisition.where: Hold a Centurion or Imperator subscription during the April 2026 promotion period to receive the helmet as a flair item. Subscriber flair carries a 3-month exclusivity window, after which CIG says such items may become earnable in-game (e.g. via loot).

sources.label: The Impound - April 2026 Subscriber Promotions
starcitizen.tools - Subscriber flair

entries.name: Calico
entries.notableFor: A lightweight, pressurized EVA-capable combat suit pitched as the go-to kit for infiltrators, snipers and thieves — people who would rather slip past a fight than kick in the door. Its signature trait is its mongrel construction: it stitches together repurposed PAB3 armor plates with a bespoke Kastak Arms helmet, giving you enough protection to shrug off some incoming fire without the bulk that would slow a stealth approach. It ships in two looks: the tan-and-brown Desert finish and the black-and-gray Tactical finish.

acquisition.where: FPS armor vendors across Stanton, Pyro and Nyx (e.g. Galleria FPS Armor shops, GrimHEX, microTech, plus Pyro outposts and jump-point gateway stations)
Lootable from NPCs and loot sites in the wild (the set is flagged as obtainable via looting)
Tactical variant can be crafted from a blueprint via the Alpha 4.x crafting system

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Calico (armor set: KSAR manufacturer, Light/pressurized, Desert & Tactical variants, buy/loot/craft flags)
Star Citizen Wiki — Calico Core Tactical (light core piece, Kastak Arms)
Star Citizen Wiki — Kastak Arms (KSAR manufacturer profile)
Cornerstone item finder — Calico Core Tactical shop locations and aUEC prices (~1,514–1,780 aUEC)

entries.name: Carrion
entries.notableFor: A medium armor set built around an outlaw/scavenger fantasy: it looks cobbled together from salvaged metal plates and ragged cloth, a crude patchwork of repurposed junk meant to read as "I'll do whatever it takes to survive." It ships in four flavor variants that lean hard into the grim aesthetic - Ashen (white paint with bone fragments worked into the plates), Payback (the same bone-studded look in aggressive red), Maniac (a mid-length cape with bones prominently integrated across several pieces), and Stalker (orange tagging, a long cape, and chunks of scavenged Vanduul armor plating bolted on). The styling ties it thematically to Grey's Market, the scrap-and-stolen-goods dealer run by Grey Chaudrey. It is pressurized, unisex, and covers helmet, core, arms, and legs.

acquisition.where: CitizenCon 2955 Digital Goodies Pack
acquisition.location: Real-money pledge bundle sold on the RSI pledge store during CitizenCon 2025; the Carrion set (and its Ashen/Maniac/Payback/Stalker variants) was included as bundle content and implemented in-game with Alpha 4.3.2. It is not currently stocked at any in-game shop/armor terminal, The CitizenCon 2025 pledge sale has ended, so it is no longer directly purchasable — the practical current source is the player marketplace (below).

acquisition.where: Player-to-player marketplaces
acquisition.location: As an account-bound-style pledge exclusive it is rarely tradable in-fiction, but community marketplaces (e.g. UEX) list player sell/buy offers for individual Carrion pieces and full sets in the ~1.2M-3M aUEC range. Treat these as unofficial player listings, not an official vendor.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Carrion (armor set)
Star Citizen Wiki - Carrion Core (manufacturer: Survivalist, datamined Alpha 4.4.0)
Star Citizen Wiki - Carrion Core Stalker (variant lore)
Star Citizen Wiki - CitizenCon 2955 Digital Goodies Pack
RSI Comm-Link - CitizenCon Direct 2955 Digital Goodies Pack Now Available
UEX - Carrion Core Stalker item page (render + marketplace listings)
Star Citizen Wiki - Grey's Market

entries.name: Dust Devil
entries.manufacturer: Unknown (scavenger-built; the "Epoque" Core variant is attributed to Survivalist)
entries.notableFor: A scavenger heavy set cobbled from welded-together pieces of salvaged, disused protective gear, giving it a brutal, improvised junker silhouette. Its signature flourish is a flowing cape repurposed from an old Banu wall-hanging, and the legs fold in salvaged ship parts paired with a decorated leather belt. The Core offers roughly 40% damage reduction across all types with strong radiation (~26,800 REM) and wide temperature tolerance, marking it as a true heavy-class loadout. A cosmetic "Epoque" recolor exists as a pledge-store cosmetic.

acquisition.where: Crafted from a blueprint at a crafting station. The Core blueprint runs about 4m20s and consumes salvage materials (e.g. Taranite, Aslarite, Riccite); the blueprint itself is unlocked through mission completion.
Drops as world loot rather than being stocked by any in-game vendor — it has no shop/kiosk listing in UEX, so it cannot be bought for aUEC in stores.
acquisition.location: Found in the persistent universe as loot

acquisition.where: The cosmetic 'Epoque' recolor is sold only via the '\''Epoque Dust Devil'\'' Patch bundle in the RSI Pledge Store (real money), bundled with the Manticore Epoque helmet, P8-SC Epoque SMG, Warden Epoque backpack, and an Intrepid paint. This is the Epoque skin only, not the standard set.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Dust Devil (set)
Star Citizen Wiki — Dust Devil Armor Core
Star Citizen Wiki — Dust Devil Armor Legs
Star Citizen Wiki — "Epoque Dust Devil" Patch bundle
star-citizen.wiki API — Dust Devil Armor Core (stats, craft, loot)
star-citizen.wiki API — Dust Devil Armor Core blueprint inputs

entries.name: DustUp
entries.notableFor: A medium-weight set built by Kastak Arms around a CDS undersuit (the same one issued to UEE Marines) with reconstructed Omni-Role mkII plating layered on for solid protection against incoming fire. Its real standout is the modified helmet, engineered to hold up against explosions and to keep its seal in vacuum, so the whole rig is pressurized and EVA-safe. The look is a rugged, utilitarian Marine-derived kit with a worn, field-tested feel, and it ships in several finishes (base Core, plus Tactical, Desert, Scorched and Firestarter variants).

acquisition.where: Bought from gear/armor shops across Stanton, Pyro and Nyx. Cheapest at roughly 3,647 aUEC from Armor stores at the ARC-L1, CRU-L1 and CRU-L4 stations and at the Jump Gateway stations (Terra/Nyx/Stanton/Pyro Gateways). Higher prices (around 4,076-4,290 aUEC) at Pyro Gateway, Skutters on GrimHEX, FPS Shop at Astors Clearing, FPS Shop at Dunboro, the Shop Terminal at Zephyr, and Frostbite. Prices and stock are per-component (Core/torso, helmet, arms, legs) and vary by patch.
acquisition.location: Multiple stations and outposts in Stanton, Pyro and Nyx

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - DustUp
UEX - DustUp Core Tactical (shops & prices)
Fextralife - DustUp Medium Armor

entries.name: Inquisitor
entries.notableFor: A medium combat set from weapon-maker Kastak Arms that leans hard into an aggressive, menacing silhouette. It pairs strong-but-lightweight alloy plating with reinforced polyfiber weaving, so it protects without weighing the wearer down. That mix of mobility and a deliberately intimidating profile is why the set became a recognizable, near-infamous look across the 'verse. It comes in a wide spread of color variants (Black Steel, Raven, White, Violet, Neon Pink, and many leg/arm colorways), with the Inquisitor Core as the torso piece.

acquisition.where: Bought at general FPS armor / gear-up equipment terminals. The Inquisitor Core (torso) runs around 3,600+ aUEC; full sets are assembled from separately-sold core, arms, legs and helmet pieces.
acquisition.location: Pyro Gateway station (Galleria FPS Armor shop) and other armor/distribution-center terminals across Stanton and Pyro

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Inquisitor (armor set)
Star Citizen Wiki - Inquisitor Core (torso piece, Kastak Arms)
UEX - Inquisitor Core item / pricing
UEX - FPS Armor terminal, Pyro Gateway

entries.name: Lynx
entries.notableFor: A light combat suit built by Kastak Arms around a high-tech polymer weave that soaks up hits while keeping the wearer fast and agile for quick getaways. Its real claim to fame is cosmetic heritage: the suit's distinctive silhouette became iconic after it was worn by the character known as "the Seeker" in the wildly popular in-fiction "Fire and Fury" vids of the mid-2800s, which is why players still chase the look today.

acquisition.where: Bought as individual pieces (Core / Arms / Legs) from gun and armor vendors. Cheapest in Pyro weapon shops, slightly pricier at Stanton orbital/gateway armor stores.
acquisition.location: Pyro: Guns shops at Starlight Service Station, Patch City, Megumi Refueling, Endgame, Rod's Fuel 'N Supplies, and Orbituary (Bloom) ~1,311 aUEC per piece. Stanton: Armor stores at Everus Harbor (Hurston) ~1,384 aUEC and ARC-L1/ARC-L4/CRU-L1 and other gateway stations ~1,456 aUEC per piece.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Lynx (armor set)
Star Citizen Wiki — Lynx Core Base (manufacturer: Kastak Arms)
UEX — Lynx Core Base (shops & prices)

entries.name: Microid Battle Suit
entries.notableFor: One of the original entry-level light armor sets and a staple starter kit for new players. Its signature look pairs ceramic plates over the chest, shoulders and shins with an exposed microweave bodysuit for the rest, giving it a slim, lightly-armored, highly mobile silhouette rather than the bulky look of medium and heavy sets. The base set is unisex, pressurized for vacuum, and pairs with the Scaleweave Undersuit. It ships in a wide range of colorways (Nomad, Badland, Lodestone, Crucible and more), making it one of the most commonly seen FPS armor sets across the verse.

acquisition.where: Found as loot on NPC bodies and inside lootable containers at ground sites; the base (uncolored) Microid pieces are not reliably stocked at tracked vendor shops in the current patch, so looting is the most consistent way to grab the plain set.
acquisition.location: Stanton & Pyro ground POIs (outposts, bunkers, derelicts)

acquisition.where: The base Microid pieces can be produced via crafting blueprints; reported component requirements include resources such as Taranite and Aslarite.

Colorway variants of the set (e.g. Microid Battle Suit Nomad helmet/core/arms/legs) are sold at outpost gear shops for roughly 1,184 aUEC per piece. Confirmed vendor locations for the Nomad variant include Sacren's Plot (Pyro IV), Shepherd's Rest (Bloom), Kabir's Post (Ignis), Jackson's Swap (Monox) and Canard View (Terminus).

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Microid Battle Suit (set page, description, classification, components)
Star Citizen Wiki - Microid Battle Suit Core
Star Citizen Wiki - Microid Battle Suit Helmet
star-citizen.wiki item DB - Microid Battle Suit Helmet Nomad (Kastak Arms, light armor, shop locations & 1,184 aUEC price)

entries.name: Monde
entries.notableFor: A designer take on heavy armor from Kilgore & Poole. The Monde stacks triple-laminate plating over modern defensive tech but keeps it wearable through a comfort-focused liner and dedicated flex-point joints at the articulation areas, so it moves better than its bulk suggests. The look is the selling point: a sleek, premium, almost fashion-forward silhouette rather than rugged military kit, and it ships in a wide spread of finishes and camo editions (Keystone white/grey/blue with URW logos, the clean Hiro white-on-grey, Crimson and Purgatory camo, Hemlock green, Daimyo, Delta, HighSec). Full set is helmet, core/torso, arms and legs, with matching backpacks on the premium bundles.

acquisition.where: In-game loot in the live universe
acquisition.location: Surfaces as facility / station loot in contested and security-breaker locations (community guides cite QV Breaker stations and Nyx armor-farming routes); base set is also obtainable via crafting blueprints

acquisition.where: Player crafting
acquisition.location: Craftable from Monde blueprints (per wiki Crafting/Blueprints entry); base set route, no pledge purchase required

acquisition.where: RSI Pledge Store (real money) for the named cosmetic editions
acquisition.location: robertsspaceindustries.com pledge/Gear — e.g. the Monde 'Keystone' Armor Set / Keystone Gear Pack; certain finishes (HighSec) tie to monthly subscriber-style bundles rather than in-game loot

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Monde
Star Citizen Wiki — Monde Core (set piece, manufacturer KAP / Kilgore and Poole)
api.star-citizen.wiki — Monde Core HighSec (Heavy, Common, v4.8.2-LIVE, Kilgore & Poole)
RSI Pledge Store — Monde 'Keystone' Armor Set
GGWTB — Nyx 4.7 Armor Farming Guide (Monde loot route)

entries.name: Morozov-SH
entries.notableFor: A rugged, battlefield-stalwart heavy set defined by its thick fortified plating and a tall, distinctive protective collar that wraps the neck. RRS developed it with input from security professionals, pairing the heavy outer armor with anti-rip protective padding and a lightweight inner lining so the suit stays comfortable and mobile despite its bulk. It is pressurized/sealed, carries generous storage, and accepts large weapon attachments, giving it the look and role of a frontline trooper rig. The set spawned a wide family of color variants and editions (Red Alert, Aftershock, Brushdrift, Thule, Redshift) plus an industrial Morozov-SH-I line built for workers in hostile zones.

acquisition.where: Pyro shops — Gear Up (Orbituary, Bloom), Shop Terminal (Blackrock Exchange, Terminus), Shop Terminal (Frigid Knot, Bloom) and Gear Up (Ruin Station, Terminus). Core piece runs roughly 8,629 UEC.
Stanton FPS gear terminals — armor shops at the ARC-L4, CRU-L1 and HUR-L1 rest stops plus storefronts on microTech and Hurston. Pricing sits a bit higher (Core around 9,587 UEC).

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Morozov-SH
UEX Corp — Morozov-SH Core (shops, prices, manufacturer)
RSI Pledge Store — RRS Morozov-SH Red Alert Armor & LMG Kit

entries.name: Novikov Exploration Suit
entries.notableFor: Heavy environmental suit built to survive extreme cold (rated to about -225C with the Novikov Helmet). UEX lists ~25% damage reduction, a -225C to 75C operating range and very high radiation protection (~33,600 REM capacity), making it a top pick for hazardous exploration and EVA work.

acquisition.where: Buyable for ~15,300-16,100 aUEC. Sold at Cargo Center Supplies (Everus Harbor, Baijini Point, Port Tressler) and jump-point cargo terminals (Pyro/Nyx Gateways), the Armor shop at Port Tressler, and Skutters at Grim HEX.
acquisition.location: Everus Harbor / Port Tressler / Grim HEX / gateways

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Novikov Exploration Suit
UEX - Novikov Exploration Suit (shops, prices, stats)

entries.name: ORC-mkX
entries.notableFor: Latest entry in CDS's Omni-Role Combat (ORC) armor line. EVA-rated suit using new EdgeCore composite-weave technology to maximize protection while staying mobile. Frequently cited as a reliable all-round bought combat set (commonly called heavy by players, officially medium).

acquisition.where: Buyable from Cubby Blast in Area18 (ArcCorp) - cheapest at ~2,721 aUEC for the Core - and from Tammany & Sons at Lorville plus most jump-point Armor shops (Pyro/Terra/Nyx/Stanton Gateways, MIC-L4, Seraphim) at ~3,000-3,200 aUEC. The older mkV runs ~1,001 aUEC (Tammany & Sons Lorville, Everus Harbor).
acquisition.location: Cubby Blast - Area18; gateway Armor shops

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - ORC-mkX
UEX - ORC-mkX Core Singularity (shops & prices)
UEX - ORC-mkV Arms Black (shops & prices)

entries.name: Palatino
entries.notableFor: A pressurized "Battle Tech" heavy set with an unmistakable tech-industrial silhouette - chunky, segmented plating crossed with exposed mechanical detailing. Quirinus Tech's pitch is that it layers reinforced plates over patented lightweight, flexible composites, so you stay mobile while soaking serious fire (the Core variant runs roughly 40% damage reduction with strong radiation and thermal protection). The base set wears green-and-black with orange accents, and it ships in a wide palette of recolors (Daystar, Metropolis, Moonfall, Necropolis, Sunstone, plus the limited Deadlock and Shadow Gild).

acquisition.where: Hostile Distribution Centers - loot the fixed orange supply boxes; pieces spawn individually (helmet, core, arms, legs, backpack)
acquisition.location: Distribution Centers on Hurston and microTech (Stanton system)

acquisition.where: Crafted via the in-game crafting system (base and most variants are flagged craftable; Core recipe uses Laranite, Aslarite and Savrilium)
Mark I variant only - obtained through the Wikelo "Test Armor" contract
Limited-time variants: Deadlock from the "Deadlock" Patch bundle and Shadow Gild from the "Shadow Gild" Monthly (subscriber) bundle

sources.label: Palatino - Star Citizen Wiki
Palatino Core (item DB, stats & description) - Star Citizen Wiki API
Palatino Armor Farming Guide: Fast Full Set Loot Run - GGWTB
How to Loot the Full Palatino Armor Set in Star Citizen 4.8 - StarZen

entries.name: Pembroke Exploration Suit
entries.notableFor: The heat counterpart to the Novikov. Built to withstand inhospitable high-temperature environments (commonly cited up to ~225C) with an advanced sensor suite that auto-adjusts to keep body temperature stable and a long-endurance life support system. Player reports cite ~40% damage reduction. Ideal for volcanic/hot-planet exploration.

acquisition.where: Bought in-game at gear/cargo terminals (component pieces tracked on UEX, e.g. Pembroke helmets ~11.1k aUEC, backpacks ~8.1k aUEC); exact full-suit terminal list was not cleanly confirmed in sources. RSI editions (Graphite/Ivory/Sunburst) are Subscriber-Store only and not buyable in-game for aUEC.
acquisition.location: in-game gear terminals (Stanton); RSI Subscriber Store for special liveries

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Pembroke Exploration Suit
Star Citizen Wiki (Fandom) - Pembroke Armor
Star Citizen Wiki (Fandom) - Pembroke RSI Graphite Edition (subscriber)

entries.name: Stitcher
entries.notableFor: A "make-do-and-mend" medium armor set built around a deliberately ramshackle, patched-together look — like it's held together with tape and good intentions, yet still rated space-safe. Signature touches include a funky double collar, generous external storage pouches, and a ribbed industrial hose running into a metal cylinder mounted on the back, selling the scavenged-survivalist Pyro-outlaw aesthetic. Core piece offers roughly 30% damage reduction across physical, energy, distortion, thermal and biological types plus radiation protection, and supports light/medium backpacks.

acquisition.where: Outpost and settlement shop terminals throughout the Pyro system (around 3,452 aUEC for the Core; arms/legs sold alongside)
acquisition.location: Canard View (Terminus), Chawla's Beach / Kabir's Post / Sacren's Plot (Pyro V), Jackson's Swap & Sunset Mesa (Monox), Bueno Ravine (Bloom)

acquisition.where: Looted from medium outlaw NPCs who wear it as part of their loadout, e.g. during the Boarding Action in Progress mission aboard an 890 Jump (periodically available in the ArcCorp and microTech systems when you have no CrimeStat)
acquisition.location: Pyro outlaw NPCs / Boarding Action mission

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Stitcher (armor set)
Star Citizen Wiki — Stitcher Core
UEX — Stitcher Core (shops & price)

entries.name: Strata
entries.notableFor: Greycat Industrial's industrial-grade medium suit built for miners but rugged enough for any expedition. The look is pure utilitarian worker: angular, precisely paneled plates that concentrate coverage where it matters, a tool belt with pouches and a reinforced storage backpack. Its signature is the helmet, whose acclaimed design has been imitated by rival manufacturers but is prized for premium materials and a next-gen air-filtration system that keeps breathing clean in deep mines or hostile atmospheres. In the live game it is best known as the Stanton mega-corp branded prize from the Second Life Resource Drive, with each of the four corporations (Hurston, ArcCorp, Crusader, microTech) getting its own corporate livery, plus a yellow-orange Greycat Industrial edition.

acquisition.where: Second Life Resource Drive dynamic event — earn the mega-corp-branded Strata set by ranking up Personal Progress with the corporation you support; the base branded variant unlocks at Tier 2 and the black variant at Tier 5 (e.g. Strata 'Hurston' Black for the Hurston team).
acquisition.location: Second Life Resource Drive (limited-time global event, Stanton)

acquisition.where: Other Strata sets/variants (including the Greycat Industrial edition) are sold on the RSI pledge store for real money, the Greycat edition bundled with a matching backpack, VOLT Pulse pistol and an ARGO MOLE paint.

Branded Strata pieces and full sets also trade hand-to-hand on the player marketplace (e.g. UEX listings such as the Hurston Dynamics full set ~1.4M UEC and a Pyrotechnic edition ~1.2M UEC); in-game armor shops in Lorville/New Babbage rotate Strata stock by patch — verify current vendor availability in-game.
acquisition.location: Player marketplace / city armor shops (Lorville, New Babbage)

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Strata
Star Citizen Wiki — Second Life Resource Drive (event & rewards)
Star Citizen Wiki — Strata Core Greycat Edition
UEX Marketplace — Strata branded armor sets (shop/price reference)

entries.name: Tailwind 'Murray Cup' Set (DefenseCon 2956 Twitch Drops)
entries.notableFor: A two-piece Tailwind helmet + flight suit colorway given out as a Twitch Drop during DefenseCon 2956 / Invictus 2026. There is no carry-over after the window, so it can no longer be earned this way.

acquisition.where: During the DefenseCon 2956 Twitch Drops campaign (May 14-27, 2026), link RSI + Twitch accounts and accumulate 4 hours of watch time on Drops-enabled streams, then claim the Tailwind Helmet + Flight Suit 'Murray Cup' to your RSI account. Campaign has now closed.

sources.label: The Impound - DefenseCon 2956 Twitch Drops Rewards & FAQ
RSI Comm-Link - Countdown to DefenseCon 2956

entries.name: Antium Heavy Armor
entries.notableFor: One of the highest-protection loot sets in 4.8 - strong environmental/radiation and ballistic resistance. The pristine White Antium and rare Moss Camo variants are especially prized as endgame-tier finds.

acquisition.where: Looted from orange and beige military containers - orange crates yield Antium gear + aUEC, beige crates hold the highest-rarity variants. Best farmed at orbital station storage rooms / supervisor offices (access cards required) and at PAF (Planetary Alignment Facility) loot sites (Hathor, Daymar) via the blue-then-red keycard loop.

sources.label: MMOPixel - SC 4.8 Weapons & Armor Changes (Antium)
MMOPixel - SC 4.8 PAF Farming Guide
Solar Trade Alliance - Armor Set Loot Locations (Antium)

entries.name: CDF Reputation Armor Set (Return of XenoThreat)
entries.type: event-reward medium combat armor (helmet + flight suit + chest)
entries.notableFor: Bespoke CDF-branded set earned only by grinding reputation during the recurring XenoThreat fleet-battle event. Not sold in any shop; account-bound flair-style reward delivered after the event closes.

acquisition.where: Take part in the Return of XenoThreat dynamic event in 4.8 and earn CDF reputation. Lower tiers unlock individual pieces (e.g. the Lamont Umbra Undersuit/flight suit at an early reputation rank); reaching the top reputation standing ('Veteran First Responder') unlocks the exclusive helmet, flight suit and chest piece plus a Spectrum badge. Rewards are attributed to your RSI account in the week after the event ends.

sources.label: MMOPixel - 4.8 Return of XenoThreat reward guide
RSI Spectrum - XenoThreat Returns: CDF Reputation Rewards
Spaceloop - XenoThreat Returns CDF Rewards

entries.name: Carnifex Armor
entries.notableFor: Aggressive outlaw boss armor - homemade-style suit offering only marginal protection but maximum attitude, decked with skulls and intimidating accessories. Prized as a rare PvP trophy from a contested-zone boss.

acquisition.where: Dropped by the Checkmate Station Contested Zone boss (boss spawn rotates with the Pyrotechnic). High-risk PvP area - come prepared.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Carnifex
SC Survival Guide - Rare Weapons and Armor (Carnifex)
MMOPixel - SC 4.8 Best Loot Farming Guide (Carnifex/Pyrotechnic boss)

entries.name: Clash
entries.notableFor: A scrappy, junk-bucket medium combat set with an unmistakable outlaw-pirate silhouette. Lore frames it as gear the Dredge were strong-armed into mass-producing, after which raiders started stealing it everywhere, so it reads as patched-together hardware rather than a clean military rig. The Core carries no integrated helmet, leaning into the improvised look, while still offering a balanced roughly 30% damage cut across physical, energy, distortion, thermal and biochemical hits without sacrificing much mobility. Introduced back in Alpha 3.4.0 as the original Shipjacker (Clash) set.

acquisition.where: Pyro outposts and hostile bunkers

No official NPC vendor sells it (flagged 'Not Sold directly or at all'); pieces appear only as combat/loot drops, frequently sharing the same loot pool as the Righteous set. The ~500K UEC figure seen online is a player-to-player UEX marketplace listing, not a fixed shop price.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Clash (armor set)
Star Citizen Wiki - Clash Core
Star Citizen Wiki - Doomsday (manufacturer)
CStone Item Finder - Clash Core (class, 30% DR, not sold)
Solar Trade Alliance - Armor Set Loot Locations (Pyro outposts/bunkers, Righteous pool)

entries.name: Corbel Heavy Industrial Armor
entries.notableFor: Heavy industrial set (added in 4.3.1) valued for its rugged look and protection; a full-set loot grind tied to the Onyx research facilities.

acquisition.where: Looted at ASD Onyx Research Facilities - specifically the Research Wing at Site B, with several loot boxes scattered around to assemble the complete set.

sources.label: SC Survival Guide - Rare Weapons and Armor (Corbel)
MMOPixel - SC Armor with Bonuses (Corbel Heavy Industrial)

entries.name: Geist Stealth Armor (ASD Edition)
entries.notableFor: The flagship stealth set. Military-grade carbon-silk weave with hair-thin filaments masks the wearer's radar/emissions signature, and tubing built into the boot soles dampens footstep noise. The ASD Edition is the sought-after black-camo-with-red-accent operative variant. Lightweight, trades protection for low detectability.

acquisition.where: Looted at ASD Onyx Research Facilities (Engineering and Research wings) via the facility missions/storyline; full set is assembled from loot boxes scattered through the site. The ASD Edition is not sold at in-game terminals.
Only otherwise obtainable player-to-player on community marketplaces (UEX/sc-market) at highly variable prices (helmet alone ranged ~80k aUEC to multi-million); no fixed shop stock.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Geist Stealth
RSI Community Hub - Full Loot Guide: ASD/Geist Stealth Armour
Solar Trade Alliance - Armor Set Loot Locations
UEX - Geist Armor Helmet ASD Edition (marketplace pricing)

entries.name: Lamont Umbra Undersuit
entries.type: event-reward undersuit / flight suit (CDF reputation tier)
entries.notableFor: Early-tier reward of the XenoThreat CDF reputation track - a distinctive dark undersuit handed out before the full set, so it is reachable at a lower reputation rank than the helmet/chest.

acquisition.where: Reach roughly reputation rank 2 with the CDF during the Return of XenoThreat event. Awarded to your RSI account after the event concludes.

sources.label: Fandom - Lamont Umbra Undersuit
RSI Spectrum - XenoThreat Returns: CDF Reputation Rewards

entries.name: ORC-mkV
entries.notableFor: An earlier-generation Omni-Role Combat suit from Clark Defense Systems and one of the most recognizable workhorse medium sets in the verse. Its calling card is a chunky, no-nonsense military silhouette - thick composite plates layered over a fiber-reinforced polymer shell that reads as old-school soldier rather than sleek operator. CDS leans on its "centuries on the battlefield" reputation, so the ORC-mkV is positioned as the dependable, durable option: enough protection for high-risk fights while still allowing a decent range of movement. A full set is assembled from the TCS-4 Undersuit, G-2 Helmet, the ORC-mkV Core, CSP-68M Backpack, and the ORC-mkV Arms and Legs, and it ships in a wide spread of color and edition variants (Grey, Black, Tan, Orange, Dark Red, microTech/Covalex/Greycat editions, Executive, etc.).

acquisition.where: Armor shops across Stanton, Pyro and Nyx
acquisition.location: Bought piece-by-piece from in-game armor vendors. The Core sells for roughly 3,040-3,200 aUEC at Tammany & Sons (Metro Center, Lorville), the Pyro Gateway armor shop, and the dedicated Armor stores at MIC-L4, Seraphim Station (Crusader), Terra Gateway, Nyx Gateway and Stanton Gateway. Arms, Legs, Undersuit, Helmet and Backpack are stocked alongside it at the same general-armor outlets.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - ORC-mkV (armor set)
Star Citizen Wiki - ORC-mkV Core (manufacturer CDS, set components)
UEX - ORC-mkV Core vendor locations & prices
Cornerstone (CStone) item finder - ORC-mkV Core

entries.name: Overlord Armor Set
entries.type: Light armor set (multi-piece; variant cores span weight classes)
entries.notableFor: A menacing, doomsday-styled outlaw kit modeled on the gear worn by the Pyro-based Overlords vigilante group — aggressive composite plating with a high, reinforced collar that shields the neck and back of the head, and high-strength synthetic fabric at the joints for mobility. It is a collector's curiosity rather than a standard gear-shop set: it shipped only as Subscriber Flair across six color variants (Predator, Supernova, Dust Storm, Riptide, Stinger, Switchback), and the helmet, arms, core, and legs were handed out in different months, so a full matching look must be pieced together from several drops. Weight class is inconsistent across the wiki: the set is presented here as light per the brief, but at least the Predator core is documented as a heavy core, so the effective class varies by piece.

acquisition.where: RSI Subscriber-exclusive store (Subscriber Flair), not sold at in-game gear shops
acquisition.location: Roberts Space Industries pledge store, subscribers-only section

acquisition.where: Originally distributed to subscribers as flair across multiple Subscriber Promotions: helmets in April 2020, three armor sets in May 2020, and additional variants (e.g. Predator core) in December 2020
acquisition.location: Real-money/account-tied subscriber rewards — no in-universe vendor or aUEC purchase

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Overlord (set overview, subscriber flair, 6 variants)
Star Citizen Wiki — Overlord Core Predator (Survivalist manufacturer, heavy core, subscriber store acquisition, Dec 2020)
Star Citizen Wiki — Overlord Helmet (Supernova variant page)
RSI Comm-Link — May 2950 Subscriber Promotions (Overlord armor sets)
RSI Comm-Link — December 2950 Subscriber Promotions (additional Overlord variants)

entries.name: Righteous / Justified (Pyro derelict outpost armor)
entries.notableFor: Distinctive medium/heavy sets that spawn as rare rolls in Pyro's derelict outpost loot crates. Justified is somewhat more common; Righteous is a rarer crate spawn. Also reported alongside Justice/Rectitude-series pieces.

acquisition.where: Run equipment/loot crates at the unmarked, abandoned derelict outposts in the Pyro system (free-loot, but PvP-risky). Heavily RNG-dependent - expect multiple empty runs between rare armor spawns.

sources.label: Solar Trade Alliance - Armor Set Loot Locations
MMOPixel - 4.8 Pyro Outpost Looting Guide

entries.name: Tactical Strike Group Reward Blueprints (Racing flight suits, Tailwind helmets, bomber jacket)
entries.notableFor: The 4.8 Tactical Strike Group is a large group mission whose completion can drop flight-suit/helmet blueprints (racing suits, Tailwind helmets), a unique bomber jacket, medium/heavy armor, FPS weapon blueprints (Novia Crossbow variants) and ship-component blueprints. Rewards are one random blueprint from a reward pool, so specific items require repeat runs.

acquisition.where: Reach InterSec Rank 3 in the Nyx system, accept 'Tactical Strike Group Needed' from MobiGlas verified contracts, and complete the multi-phase operation (a large group is recommended). Completion grants aUEC plus a random blueprint/reward from the pools.

sources.label: ExpCarry - 4.8 Tactical Strike Guide
RSI Comm-Link - Alpha 4.8 Tactical Strike
starcitizen.tools - Tactical Strike Group

entries.name: Testudo
entries.notableFor: A clean, military-styled medium armor line that pairs tough-but-lightweight plating with ultra-strong synthetic fibers and a moisture-wicking micromodal lining, giving balanced protection without the bulk of heavy plate. It is best known for its huge spread of finishes and camo patterns (Turfwar, Nightveil, Combustion, Earthshake, Deathblow, Disrupt/Purgatory Camo and more), letting players run a consistent tactical silhouette across many color schemes. The standout is the special "Clanguard" version, a Wikelo Emporium reward built by trading in a full Turfwar set plus salvaged Vanduul materials.

acquisition.where: Recovered from armor crates and weapon/armor boxes while clearing combat sites in Alpha 4.8 (e.g. Planetary Alignment Facilities and similar bunker/facility loot runs); prioritize the larger armor crates and orange Antium boxes.
acquisition.location: Planetary Alignment Facilities (PAF) and other contested combat facilities

acquisition.where: Buildable from a recovered/blueprint recipe at a crafting bench, using refined materials such as Ouratite, Aslarite and Stileron.

The special 'Clanguard' variant is a Wikelo Emporium project reward: accept the collection mission at the Emporium bazaar, then deposit the required ingredients via the freight elevator (a full Testudo Turfwar set — Helmet, Core, Arms, Legs, Backpack — plus 15 Vanduul Plating and 15 Vanduul Metal).

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Testudo (set)
Star Citizen Wiki — Testudo Arms
star-citizen.wiki API — Testudo Core (Quirinus Tech, medium armor, craft/loot)
Wikelo Emporium tracker — Testudo Clanguard recipe (Turfwar set + Vanduul Plating/Metal)
GGWTB — SC 4.8 rare armor / PAF farm loot guide
Testudo Core product render (image)

entries.name: Wrecker
entries.manufacturer: Survivalist (SRVL) — the individual Wrecker pieces are datamined as Survivalist; note the set's wiki infobox lists the manufacturer code GLSN, so the maker attribution is not fully consistent across sources.
entries.notableFor: A grimy, salvaged outlaw heavy set built from discarded metal fused with pilfered fabric and "anointed" with bone fragments plus scavenged Vanduul armor plates, giving it a brutal trophy-hunter look. The fully enclosed helmet is its signature: an intimidating mask with four red eye-like sensors. Strongly associated with Grey's Market and the most ruthless bounty hunters. Comes in three finishes — standard (orange spray), Ashen (white), and Payback (red).

acquisition.where: Looted from outlaw/NPC corpses and armor crates rather than bought from a standard armor terminal. Community farming guides point to wreck sites, the Onyx abandoned-research facilities, and Planetary Alignment Facilities (PAFs) around Crusader's moons (e.g. Daymar) as reliable spawn spots for Wrecker pieces in current patches.
acquisition.location: Wreck sites / Onyx Facilities / PAF loot rooms (Stanton system)

acquisition.where: Community guides cite the 'Retrieve Additional Smuggler Intel' contract line from Intersec Defense Solutions as a repeatable farm whose target stations spawn Wrecker armor; raising IDS reputation increases armor spawn frequency. Treat as a player-reported farm, not an official guaranteed reward.

The SC Wiki notes the Wrecker set 'can be assumed to be sold by Grey's Market' (the grey-market outlaw vendor run by Grey Chaudrey). This is an unconfirmed assumption on the wiki, not a verified in-game purchase terminal or price.

The red 'Payback' color variant of the full Wrecker set (Helmet, Core, Arms, Legs) is a real-money, time-limited pledge item sold via the RSI pledge store as part of the CitizenCon Direct 2955 Digital Goodies Pack — it is not traded in-game.
acquisition.location: RSI Pledge Store — CitizenCon Direct 2955 Digital Goodies Pack

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Wrecker (set page)
Star Citizen Wiki — Wrecker Core (datamine, Survivalist mfr)
Star Citizen Wiki — Wrecker Core Ashen variant
Star Citizen Wiki — Grey's Market vendor
UEX — Wrecker Core Payback item (stats, CitizenCon 2955 pledge, render image)
MMOPIXEL — Star Citizen Wrecker Armor Farming Guide (loot locations)
MMOPIXEL — SC 4.8 Best Loot Farming Guide (Onyx Facilities / PAFs)
RSI — CitizenCon Direct 2955 Digital Goodies Pack (Payback pledge availability)

entries.name: Cambio-Lite SRT Attachment
entries.notableFor: The handheld salvage-and-repair head for the Pyro RYT: strips hull coating into a canister, then uses that stored material for quick field hull repairs. Lets a solo player salvage and patch ships without a Vulture/Reclaimer. Also craftable at filler stations aboard salvage ships.

acquisition.where: Platinum Bay - Everus Harbor and Tammany & Sons - Lorville (cheapest, ~357 UEC)
Pyro Refinery Shops (Checkmate, Orbituary, Ruin Station) ~360 UEC
Most FPS/Refinery shops standard ~375 UEC (76+ locations, patch 4.8.2)
Craftable at filler stations aboard the Vulture / Reclaimer for a percentage of salvage material

sources.label: UEX - Cambio-Lite SRT Attachment (price + buy locations, v4.8.2)
Star Citizen Wiki - Cambio-Lite SRT Attachment

entries.name: MaxLift Tractor Beam
entries.notableFor: The go-to handheld tractor-beam gun for cargo work: moves objects up to ~16 SCU at 10-20 m range, far stronger than the multi-tool TruHold attachment. Can also pull yourself toward objects in EVA, and two MaxLifts combined can lift up to 32 SCU. Listed under Personal Weapons in shop terminals.

acquisition.where: Cargo Center Supplies - Everus Harbor (~18,050 UEC) and Tammany & Sons - Lorville (~18,216 UEC), cheaper than average
Cousin Crow's (Orison), Cubby Blast (Area18), Dumper's Depot (Area18/GrimHEX), Platinum Bay, Kel-To (New Babbage) at ~19,000-19,175 UEC
Lootable crates at mining/research ground stations sometimes contain tractor-beam gear

sources.label: UEX - MaxLift Tractor Beam (price + 100+ buy locations)
Star Citizen Wiki - MaxLift Tractor Beam

entries.name: OreBit Mining Attachment
entries.notableFor: Turns the Pyro RYT into a handheld miner: scans rocks for worthwhile deposits and breaks them open with a mining laser. The entry point into mining without owning a mining ship or the bigger Multitool-based ore extraction.

acquisition.where: Cubby Blast (cheapest, ~319 UEC)
Tammany & Sons (~331 UEC)
Cargo Center Supplies (~357 UEC)
Platinum Bay, Omega Pro, Refinery Shops at standard ~375 UEC across Stanton, Pyro and Nyx

sources.label: UEX - OreBit Mining Attachment (price + buy locations)
Star Citizen Wiki - OreBit Mining Attachment

entries.name: Pyro RYT Multi-Tool
entries.type: modular handheld multi-tool (base unit for attachments)
entries.notableFor: The single most useful piece of personal utility gear: a cheap base handle that accepts swappable attachments to mine, salvage/repair, heal, or run a small tractor beam. One tool covers four professions if you carry the attachments.

acquisition.where: Cubby Blast (cheapest, ~416 UEC)
Tammany & Sons (~428 UEC)
Cargo Center Supplies (~461 UEC)
Platinum Bay / Refinery Shops and Omega Pro at standard ~485 UEC; stocked at 110+ locations across Stanton, Pyro and Nyx

sources.label: UEX - Pyro RYT Multi-Tool (price + buy locations)
Star Citizen Wiki - Pyro RYT Multi-Tool

entries.name: Executive Hangar / Contested Zone Loot (rare weapons, Grade-A components, gold helmet)
entries.type: contested-zone exclusive loot (FPS weapons + ship components + cosmetic gear)
entries.notableFor: The Executive Hangars / contested zones are the premier PvPvE loot endgame. Beyond armor, the red crates and reward rooms yield rare FPS weapons (grenade launchers, railguns, snipers) and Grade-A ship components. Turning in Boards 1-7 trades for ships and high-tier gear. Loot frequently fails to spawn, so it is heavily RNG- and patch-dependent.

acquisition.where: Enter the contested zones / executive hangars (notably on the Pyro stations). Loot red crates and reward rooms, or collect Board sets (1 through 7) and turn them in for ships, powerful FPS weapons and Grade-A components. On contested servers the advice in 4.8 is to grab one strong armor set + a weapon and extract rather than fully clear.
acquisition.location: Pyro station contested zones / executive hangars

sources.label: starcitizen.tools - Guide:Executive Hangars
starcitizen.tools - Contested Zones
MMOPixel - Free rare/legendary items guide

entries.name: Volt Zenith Sniper Rifle (Onyx loot)
entries.notableFor: Onyx-facility loot weapon that pairs with the Geist stealth set - combines charged sniper shots with rapid-fire marksman capability; the scope tracks charge and heat, and overheating locks you into a cooldown animation.

acquisition.where: Loot ASD Onyx facilities (same bases as Geist armor). The Volt Zenith spawns in storage rooms, security rooms, gantries and lockers - check lockers in particular while running the Geist orange-box route.

sources.label: MMOPixel - 4.3 ASD Onyx Facilities Guide
MMOPixel - 4.3 All Features Guide

entries.name: LifeGuard Medical Attachment
entries.notableFor: First-aid head for the Pyro RYT, a Greycat/CureLife joint product: heals players and NPCs by dispensing Hemozal. Lighter and cheaper than the dedicated MedGun/ParaMed but lacks detailed diagnostics. Sold at pharmacies/medical shops, not weapon stores.

acquisition.where: Pharmacy - Maria Pure of Heart (Lorville) and Pharmacy - Everus Harbor (cheapest, ~333 UEC)
Empire Hospital Pharmacy (Area18), Orison General Hospital, and Lagrange-point pharmacies at standard ~350 UEC
Medical Shops at Checkmate, Orbituary, Patch City, Ruin Station (Pyro); pharmacies at Levski (Nyx); Skutters at GrimHEX

sources.label: UEX - LifeGuard Medical Attachment (price + buy locations)
Star Citizen Wiki - LifeGuard Medical Attachment

entries.name: MedPen (Hemozal)
entries.notableFor: The essential pocket heal: a single-use auto-injector that dispenses the coagulant Hemozal to stop bleeding and stabilize vitals in emergencies. Standard loadout staple every player should carry. Widely stocked at pharmacies and weapon shops.

acquisition.where: Guns - Checkmate (cheapest, ~239 UEC) and Tammany & Sons - Lorville (~249 UEC)
Most pharmacies and Refinery Shops at standard ~265 UEC (159+ locations across Stanton, Pyro, Nyx)
Cubby Blast - Area18 (~282 UEC), Live Fire Weapons - ARC-L1 (~280 UEC)

sources.label: UEX - MedPen (Hemozal) (price + buy locations)
Star Citizen Wiki - MedPen (Hemozal)

entries.name: OxyPen
entries.notableFor: A single-use auto-injector that replenishes a small amount of oxygen, a lifesaver during EVA, in depressurized wrecks/caves, or when a suit's O2 runs low. Cheap survival insurance for explorers and salvagers.

acquisition.where: Pyro medical shops (Checkmate, Starlight Service, Patch City) cheapest ~234 UEC
Pharmacies at Everus Harbor and Lagrange points (HUR-L1..L5), ArcCorp, MicroTech, Crusader ~247-260 UEC
CenterMass and Cubby Blast outlets (~275-277 UEC)

sources.label: UEX - OxyPen (price + buy locations)
Star Citizen Wiki (Fandom) - OxyPen

entries.name: ParaMed Medical Device (MedGun)
entries.type: handheld medical gun / diagnostics + multi-drug injector
entries.notableFor: The dedicated medical gun (the wiki MedGun page resolves to ParaMed). Two modes: basic (multi-tool-like first aid) and advanced (full diagnostics plus adjustable injection of Hemozal, Roxaphen, Demexatrine, Sterogen or Resurgera). Uses MedGel/ParaMed refill vials. The serious medic's tool over the LifeGuard attachment.

acquisition.where: Pharmacy - HUR-L1 (cheapest, ~1,250 UEC)
Empire Hospital Pharmacy (Area18), Pharmacy - Port Tressler, standard ~1,500 UEC
Medical Shop - Patch City (Pyro), Pharmacy - Levski (Nyx); ~49 locations total at ~1,485 UEC avg

sources.label: UEX - ParaMed Medical Device (price + buy locations)
Star Citizen Wiki - MedGun (redirects to ParaMed Medical Device)
Star Citizen Wiki - ParaMed Medical Device

entries.name: Casaba Outlet
entries.notableFor: Fashion/clothing chain found at most major landing zones - the standard source for casual clothing and undersuits (the base layer worn under armor). Present in Area18 (Retail Plaza) and other hubs.

acquisition.where: Buy casual clothing and undersuits. Branches at most major locations including Area18.
acquisition.location: Area18 Retail Plaza (ArcCorp) and other major hubs across Stanton

sources.label: Fandom - Casaba Outlet (Area18)
starcitizen.tools - Casaba Outlet
StarCitizenHelp - Shops Directory

entries.name: CenterMass
entries.notableFor: Dedicated armor-and-weapons chain. Started weapons-only and added armor to its catalog in 2939; keeps on-site mechanics for installing, modifying and repairing purchased gear. One of the most reliable one-stop stops for both armor sets and FPS weapons in Stanton.

acquisition.where: Buy personal armor and FPS weapons; the New Babbage branch is the go-to armor shop on microTech.
acquisition.location: New Babbage, The Commons / Plaza (microTech, Stanton)

acquisition.where: Second branch carrying the same armor + weapon stock.
acquisition.location: Area18, ArcCorp Plaza, 2nd floor of the IO-North Tower (ArcCorp, Stanton)

sources.label: starcitizen.tools - CenterMass
Fandom - CenterMass (Area18)
Fandom - CenterMass (New Babbage)

entries.name: Cubby Blast
entries.notableFor: Widely cited as having the broadest personal-weapon selection in Stanton - ballistic rifles, energy weapons, shotguns and pistols - and it also stocks some armor. The default destination for FPS loadouts on ArcCorp.

acquisition.where: Buy ballistic/energy rifles, shotguns, pistols and some armor pieces.

sources.label: starcitizen.tools - Cubby Blast
CStone Finder - Cubby Blast (Area18)
StarCitizenHelp - Shops Directory

entries.name: Garrity Defense
entries.notableFor: Historic flight-suit and light/heavy body-armor retailer. Its Port Olisar storefront was removed in Alpha 3.20.0 (Sept 2023) when Port Olisar was retired and an armor shop moved to Seraphim Station. Listed here for reference - not a current shopping destination in 4.8.

acquisition.where: No longer operating - the Port Olisar branch was removed in 3.20.0; armor sales shifted to Seraphim Station. Do not rely on Garrity Defense as a live vendor.

sources.label: starcitizen.tools - Garrity Defense
Fandom - Garrity Defense

entries.name: Providence Surplus
entries.notableFor: Crusader Industries' workforce outfitter on Orison - sells clothing, personal armor and personal equipment. The main spot to assemble a replaceable working kit on Crusader.

acquisition.where: Buy personal armor, clothing and personal equipment for the Crusader workforce.

sources.label: starcitizen.tools - Providence Surplus
CStone Finder - Providence Surplus (Orison)
boostroom - Best Places to Buy Gear

entries.name: Tammany & Sons
entries.notableFor: General-goods outfitter that bundles clothing, personal armor, weapons and equipment in one place - convenient for a full restock on Hurston. Also stocks Greycat Industrial's Pyro RYT multi-tool.

acquisition.where: Buy clothing, personal armor, weapons and the Pyro RYT multi-tool. Located in the L19 Workers District next to the miners' rail line.
acquisition.location: Lorville, L19 Workers District / Central Business District (Hurston, Stanton)

sources.label: starcitizen.tools - Tammany and Sons

entries.name: Gear Up (Pyro station gear vendors)
entries.notableFor: The generic in-station gear/armor counter found at Pyro stations - the primary way to buy personal armor, undersuits and weapons inside the Pyro system. Branches appear at Checkmate (PYR2-L4), Orbituary (above Bloom) and Ruin Station (Terminus). Stock is outlaw-leaning and drifts patch to patch; verify live inventory via item-finder tools.

acquisition.where: Buy armor, undersuits and weapons at the station's Gear Up counter.
acquisition.location: Checkmate Station (PYR2-L4), Orbituary (high orbit above Bloom), and Ruin Station (orbiting Terminus) - Pyro system

entries.name: Main Personal Gear & Armor Shops (by location)
entries.type: shop directory - where to buy armor / weapons / utility gear generally
entries.notableFor: Quick reference for where to shop for armor, weapons and utility gear in Stanton. Cubby Blast (Area18) has the widest personal-weapon selection plus flak jackets and Titan-suit servos; Tammany & Sons (Lorville) is one of the most complete general stores for armor/gear/weapons; New Babbage (Commons) is best for mining gear and ship components; pharmacies and medical shops carry MedPens, OxyPens and medical devices.

acquisition.where: Cubby Blast - widest personal-weapons selection, flak jackets, military surplus armor pieces
Tammany & Sons - broad general store: armor, weapons, tools, attachments (often cheapest)
Dumper's Depot - salvage/used gear and tractor beams; Omega Pro / Kel-To - New Babbage Commons (gear + components)
Pharmacies & hospital medical shops - MedPen, OxyPen, ParaMed/MedGun, LifeGuard attachment
acquisition.location: Area18, Orison, Lorville, New Babbage + Lagrange stations

entries.name: Shubin Interstellar (mining shop)
entries.notableFor: Best one-stop shop for handheld and ship mining lasers, mining heads and related mining modules/equipment - the main outfitter if your 'gear' is mining-focused rather than combat.
